message:
feat(glamour): Add comprehensive syntax highlighting tests and table parsing module

This commit significantly enhances the glamour markdown rendering crate with
extensive test coverage and a new table parsing module.

New Table Module (table.rs):
- Add dedicated table parsing module for markdown tables
- Provides structured table parsing separate from inline rendering
- Enables better table layout control and cell alignment

Syntax Highlighting Tests (e2e_highlighting_tests):
- Add full document rendering tests with mixed code blocks (Rust, Python, JSON)
- Add theme consistency tests ensuring same tokens get same colors
- Add error resilience tests for malformed language tags, very long code
  blocks, unicode content, empty blocks, unknown languages
- Add tests verifying all available themes render without panic
- Add line numbers tests for correct count and disabled-by-default
- Add custom language alias tests
- Add performance tests for large files and many small code blocks
- Add integration tests with surrounding markdown elements

Additional Syntax Module Tests:
- Add tests for all builtin themes loading
- Add tests verifying different themes produce different output
- Add empty and whitespace-only code highlighting tests
- Add trailing newline preservation tests
- Add tab and mixed indentation handling tests
- Add unicode in strings preservation tests
- Add large file highlighting performance tests
- Add style cache efficiency tests
- Add canonical language name verification tests
- Add supported languages completeness tests (30+ languages)

Cargo.toml Updates:
- Add unicode-width dependency for table cell width calculation
- Add required-features for syntax_highlighting and theme_gallery examples
- Ensure examples only run when syntax-highlighting feature is enabled

Code Formatting:
- Apply rustfmt to examples for consistent style

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ description = "Stylesheet-based markdown rendering for terminal applications"
 [dependencies]
 lipgloss = { path = "../lipgloss" }
 pulldown-cmark = "0.13"
+unicode-width = "0.1"
 
 # Optional: syntax highlighting
 # Uses fancy-regex (pure Rust) instead of onig for WASM compatibility
@@ -37,5 +38,13 @@ stats_alloc = "0.1.10"
 name = "glamour_benchmarks"
 harness = false
 
+[[example]]
+name = "syntax_highlighting"
+required-features = ["syntax-highlighting"]
+
+[[example]]
+name = "theme_gallery"
+required-features = ["syntax-highlighting"]
+
 [lints]
 workspace = true
